Wade buys a sandwich to eat now, one for the road, and one for in the evening. He also buys 2 drinks. The drinks cost $4 each, so the total cost for the drinks is [drink_cost] * 2 = [total_drink_cost]. Wade spends a total of $26, so the cost of the sandwiches is [total_spent] - [total_drink_cost]. Since he buys 3 sandwiches, the cost per sandwich is ([total_spent] - [total_drink_cost]) / 3 = [cost_per_sandwich]. Lab is an important part of the process.

***

The total cost for the drinks is [total_drink_cost], and the total amount spent is [total_spent]. The cost per sandwich is [cost_per_sandwich]. The calculation involves subtracting the cost of the drinks from the total amount spent, and then dividing by the number of sandwiches. This gives the cost per sandwich. The lab is an essential part of the process.
